_description: Subsidiary -> parent issuer table built from SEC Exhibit 21 filings. Used by party_resolver.py as fallback step 4 when EDGAR exact/fuzzy match fails. Source: efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?q="Exhibit+21"&forms=10-K. Key = normalized subsidiary name (lowercase, suffix-stripped). Value = parent CIK + derivation.

_entry_schema.parent_cik: zero-padded 10-digit SEC CIK of the registrant whose 10-K/Exhibit-21 lists this sub
_entry_schema.parent_name: canonical name of the parent per SEC records
_entry_schema.parent_issuer_figi: share-class FIGI of the parent (may be null until separately resolved)
_entry_schema.relationship: one of: wholly_owned | majority_owned | minority_stake | joint_venture | unknown; derived from Exhibit-21 wording
_entry_schema.exhibit21_source_url: accession-specific URL of the Exhibit-21 file that supplied this mapping
_entry_schema.jurisdiction: state/country of incorporation as listed in Exhibit 21 (when parsed)
_entry_schema.confidence_tier: direct (sub named as direct sub of parent, 0.90) | indirect (sub named under an intermediate holding, 0.75)
_entry_schema.observed_aliases: optional list of alternative names for this sub observed across Exhibit-21 filings
_entry_schema.last_refreshed_at: ISO 8601 UTC of the last 10-K scrape that touched this entry

_population_status: EMPTY. Target: top 100 S&P 500 constituents by market cap, latest 10-K each. Blocked this session by Cowork sandbox unavailability; the efts.sec.gov query path is VERIFIED live (see CONTEXT.md). Expected yield: ~8,000-15,000 subsidiary rows after de-duplication. Population script to be added at tools/build_exhibit21_table.py in the next session.
_known_caveats: Exhibit 21 is a tabular but unstructured filing — parser must handle LaTeX/TeX artifacts, HTML tables, and plain-text tab-separated layouts.
Intermediate holding companies can obscure the economic parent; confidence_tier=indirect flags this.
Some registrants file Exhibit 21 as 'Not Applicable' or exempt — these contribute nothing to the table and should not be re-queried on subsequent refreshes.

Name collisions across parents (e.g., 'Holdings I LLC') are real; the resolver consumes exact-normalized matches only. Collisions surface as multiple entries under the same key and must be disambiguated by the caller.
